rulings.Case Title: DTG OPERATIONS INC., A CORPORATION VS. JANE ACAP et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Motion To Amend Judgment And Request To Enter Renewal Of Judgment Originally Filed On December 30, 2022; And Proposed Order
rulings.Rulings: Set for Law and Motion/Discovery Calendar on Friday, December 05, 2025, Line 1. Plaintiff DTG Operations Inc.'s unopposed Motion To Amend Judgment And Request To Enter Renewal Of Judgment Originally Filed On December 30, 2022, is DENIED. Plaintiff has not demonstrated that its motion to renew the judgment is timely. (See Code of Civil Procedure sections 683.020 [judgment generally enforceable for a period of ten years following entry]; 683.120 [judgment creditor may renew judgment]; 683.130 [application for renewal must be filed before expiration of 10-year period].) Here, the clerk entered judgment on January 4, 2013. Plaintiff/judgment-creditor attempted to file a request for renewal on December 30, 2022, but the filing was rejected. At no point before January 4, 2023. did Plaintiff actually file a request for renewal. The instant request is untimely. The court will not treat this motion as a motion filed December 30, 2022, or as an extension of a motion filed in December 2022-three years ago. Plaintiffs statement that it was unaware its December 2022 request for renewal was originally rejected does not change the analysis. Moving party is ordered to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the above text and email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the time set for hearing. For the 9:00 a.m. calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 302 remotely or in person. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. (Dept. 302 Zoom ID 160 409 7690; Passcode 516287.) To appear remotely at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The text of the email shall include the name and contact information, including email address, of the attorney or party who will appear at the hearing. The court no longer provides a court reporter in the Law & Motion Department. Parties may retain their own reporter, who may appear in the courtroom or remotely. A retained reporter must be a California certified court reporter (CSR), for only a CSR's transcript may be used in California courts. If a CSR is being retained, include in your email all of the following: their name, CSR and telephone numbers, and their individual work email address =(302/JMQ)

rulings.Case Title: PAUL M. CAREY JR. VS. VINCENT DABBAY ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion For Summary Judgment
rulings.Rulings: Set for Law and Motion/Discovery Calendar on Friday, December 05, 2025, Line 6. Plaintiff Paul M. Carey Jr.'s unopposed Motion for Summary Judgment is OFF CALENDAR. Plaintiff failed to file and serve the motion in compliance with the timing requirements of Code of Civil Procedure section 437c(a)(2) [81-day notice]. The court therefore cannot hear the motion. (Urshan v. Musicians' Credit Union (2004) 120 Cal.App.4th 758, 768 ["In sum, the statutory language and existing case law lead us to the conclusion a trial court does not have authority to shorten the minimum notice period for summary judgment hearings absent the express consent of the parties."].) For the 9:00 a.m. calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 302 remotely or in person. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. (Dept. 302 Zoom ID 160 409 7690; Passcode 516287.) To appear remotely at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The text of the email shall include the name and contact information, including email address, of the attorney or party who will appear at the hearing. The court no longer provides a court reporter in the Law & Motion Department. Parties may retain their own reporter, who may appear in the courtroom or remotely. A retained reporter must be a California certified court reporter (CSR), for only a CSR's transcript may be used in California courts. If a CSR is being retained, include in your email all of the following: their name, CSR and telephone numbers, and their individual work email address. =(302/JMQ)

rulings.Case Title: CAVALRY SPV I, LLC VS. MARITZA F CRUZ
rulings.Calendar Matter: Motion For Order That Matters In Request For Admissions Be Deemed Admitted Pursuant To Ccp 2033.280
rulings.Rulings: Set for Law and Motion/Discovery Calendar on Friday, December 05, 2025, Line 8. Plaintiff Cavalry SPV I, LLC's unopposed Motion For Order That Matters In Request For Admissions Be Deemed Admitted Pursuant To CCP 2033.280 is GRANTED. The record demonstrates Defendant Maritza F. Cruz did not provide any responses to Plaintiff's Request For Admissions. Set One. (See Code of Civil Procedure section 2033.280.) Defendant's failure to timely respond is not excused. No meet and confer requirement when no responses have been provided. Plaintiffs, however, conferred. Plaintiffs' RFAs, Set One are deemed admitted. For the 9:00 a.m. calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 302 remotely or in person. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. (Dept. 302 Zoom ID 160 409 7690; Passcode 516287.) rulings.Case Title: JPMORGAN CHASE BANK, N.A. VS. APRIL FOSTER
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of And Motion To Compel Arbitration And Stay Proceedings
rulings.Rulings: Set for Law and Motion/Discovery Calendar on Friday, December 05, 2025, Line 12. Defendant April Foster's motion to compel arbitration and stay is granted. The parties entered into an enforceable arbitration agreement that covers this dispute, and Plaintiff JP Morgan Chase Bank, N.A. does not substantively oppose the motion. The Parties' arbitration agreement provides: "Under this agreement to arbitrate, the party filing a Claim must select either Judicial Arbitration and Mediation Services ("JAMS") or the American Arbitration Association ('AAA") as the arbitration administrator." (Complaint, Ex. A, pg. 10.) The court compels the parties to arbitrate before AAA since plaintiff is the claimant. The court stays this action under Code of Civil Procedure section 1281.4. For the 9:00 a.m. calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 302 remotely or in person. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. (Dept. 302 Zoom ID 160 409 7690; Passcode 516287.) To appear remotely at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The text of the email shall include the name and contact information, including email address, of the attorney or party who will appear at the hearing. Counsel for the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. The court no longer provides a court reporter in the Law & Motion Department. Parties may retain their own reporter, who may appear in the courtroom or remotely. A retained reporter must be a California certified court reporter (CSR), for only a CSR's transcript may be used in California courts. If a CSR is being retained, include in your email all of the following: their name, CSR and telephone numbers, and their individual work email address. =(302/JMQ)
